title: Middlewares
description: Built-in middleware and writing your own

blocks.text: A middleware takes a Context and returns a Response — either handling the request or calling ctx.next() to pass control down the chain. Register with app.use(), optionally scoped to a path.
Import these from @hushkey/howl/middleware. Each is a factory that returns a middleware.

rows: Deduplicates concurrent identical GETs into one handler execution.
Gzip/deflate for text, JSON, JS and SVG responses.
CSRF origin validation for unsafe methods.

blocks.text: Each middleware also has its own subpath export — e.g. @hushkey/howl/middleware/cors — if you want to import just one without pulling the whole barrel.
Order matters — middleware runs top to bottom. Put request coalescing first (thundering-herd protection), then compression, then static files, then your routes.

import { coalesceRequests, compression, staticFiles } from "@hushkey/howl/middleware";

app.use(coalesceRequests()); // dedupe concurrent GETs — first
app.use(compression());      // compress everything below
app.use(staticFiles());      // serve static/
app.fsClientRoutes();        // pages

coalesceRequests only deduplicates GETs with no Cookie or Authorization header. Authenticated requests always get their own handler execution, so a user never receives another user's coalesced response.
Await ctx.next() to run logic before and after the rest of the chain. Set ctx.state for downstream handlers, or ctx.headers to add headers to the response that comes back.

Don't add auth gates as inline middleware on API routes — set ctx.state in middleware and enforce roles via checkPermissionStrategy in defineConfig, so every role-protected endpoint is covered uniformly.
The csp() middleware can emit a per-request nonce that the renderer applies to the bootloader <script>, so you can run a strict Content-Security-Policy without 'unsafe-inline'.
